translations.Enable client-side end-to-end encryption: Enable client-side end-to-end encryption for your most sensitive files, ensuring complete privacy with zero-knowledge security.
translations.## **End-to-End Encryption**\n\n### For:
## **End-to-End Encryption**

### For End Users

**Protect your most sensitive files with strong encryption.**

The End-to-End Encryption app gives you complete control over your data privacy.
With this app, you can encrypt specific folders so that only you (and those you trust) can access their contents.
Your files are encrypted on your device before they reach the server, ensuring that no one—not even the server administrator—can read them.

**Benefits:**
- 🔒 **True privacy**: Files are encrypted on your device and can only be decrypted by you
- 📱 **Works across all platforms**: Fully supported on desktop, Android, iOS clients, and as you wish even in the browser
- 🎯 **Selective encryption**: Choose which folders to encrypt
- 🛡️ **Secure sharing**: Share encrypted files with other users or even secure public upload using the encrypted file drop

---

### For Administrators

**Enterprise-ready end-to-end encryption infrastructure for your Nextcloud instance.**

This app provides all the necessary server-side APIs and infrastructure to enable End-to-End encryption (E2EE) for your users.
It ensures that encrypted data remains secure throughout its lifecycle on your server.

**Technical highlights:**
- 🔐 **Complete API suite**: Provides all client-side APIs needed for E2EE implementation
- 🔒 **Secure FileDrop integration**: Enables secure file sharing with encryption
- 🛡️ **Zero-knowledge architecture**: Server never has access to encryption keys
- ⚙️ **Group restrictions**: Limit app usage to specific user groups if needed
- 🔄 **Background job management**: Automatic rollback handling for failed operations

### Setup
This application provides the server-side infrastructure for end-to-end encryption, but it requires client support to function.
To enable end-to-end encryption, users will need to install the corresponding client-side app on their devices (desktop, Android, iOS) or use the web client.

Using the web interface, after enabling it in the personal settings, allows you to encrypt files and folders directly in the browser,
providing a seamless experience without needing additional software. But also requires some kind of trust in the server as the code is delivered by the server and could be manipulated.

Once enable through clients or the web interface, you can create encrypted folders and upload or move files into them.
The clients and the web interface will handle the encryption and decryption processes automatically.

⚠️ This comes with some limitations and caveats, as only normal file operations can be handled.
Meaning that some apps in the web interface do not work with encrypted files.
translations.The server could serve malicious source: The server could serve malicious source code to extract the secret that protects your files.
translations.Do not show this warning the next time: Do not show this warning the next time
translations.A folder with this name already exists: A folder with this name already exists
translations.\"{char}\" is not allowed in folder name: "{char}" is not allowed in folder names
translations.Encrypting your files in the browser can: Encrypting your files in the browser can weaken security
translations.To set up encryption, a new private key: To set up encryption, a new private key will be generated for you.
translations.Please make sure to back up the followin: Please make sure to back up the following recovery phrase, as it will be required to access your encrypted files.
Download and decrypt all selected files.
translations.Unlike upload-only shares view-only shar: Unlike upload-only shares view-only shares require a trusted server to enforce the restriction.
translations.Please share the secret mnemonic with th: Please share the secret mnemonic with the recipient using a secure second channel.
translations.The selected user has not yet enabled en: The selected user has not yet enabled end-to-end encryption.
translations.Share recipients always have access to t: Share recipients always have access to the full encrypted folder.
translations.It is only possible to share with accoun: It is only possible to share with accounts that have already setup end-to-end encryption.
translations.When limiting the permissions you have t: When limiting the permissions you have to trust the server to enforce the limitation.
translations.Decrypting your files in the browser can: Decrypting your files in the browser can weaken security
translations.This share is using a legacy encryption: This share is using a legacy encryption method. Please ask the share owner to update the encryption metadata.
translations.When at least one group is selected, onl: When at least one group is selected, only people of the listed groups can use the End-to-End encryption app.
translations.This is the final warning: Do you really: This is the final warning: Do you really want to reset your keys?
translations.End-to-end encryption is currently enabl: End-to-end encryption is currently enabled and correctly setup.
translations.End-to-end encryption is currently disab: End-to-end encryption is currently disabled. You can set it up with the {productName} clients.
translations.End to end encryption not available: End to end encryption not available
translations.Either your browser does not support end: Either your browser does not support end-to-end encryption or you are using an insecure connection (HTTP). Please use a modern browser and ensure you are using HTTPS.
translations.Enabling E2EE in the browser can weaken: Enabling E2EE in the browser can weaken security
translations.Please read carefully before resetting y: Please read carefully before resetting your end-to-end encryption keys
translations.Once your end-to-end encryption keys are: Once your end-to-end encryption keys are reset, all files stored in your encrypted folder will be inaccessible.
translations.You should only reset your end-to-end en: You should only reset your end-to-end encryption keys if you lost your secure key words (mnemonic).
translations.Check on all connected devices if you ca: Check on all connected devices if you can retrieve your mnemonic.
translations.Any still connected device might cause p: Any still connected device might cause problems after deleting the keys, so it is better to disconnect and reconnect the devices again.

translations.There are pending tasks that need to be: There are pending tasks that need to be completed. Are you sure you want to leave?
translations.This app provides all the necessary APIs:
This app provides all the necessary APIs to implement End-to-End encryption on the client side.
Additionally it implements Secure FileDrop and makes sure that End-to-End encrypted files are neither accessible via the web interface nor other WebDAV clients.
